metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4492
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This annotation is part of a systematic annotation of all synaptic vesicle V-ATPases.

Rationale: The luminal V-ATPase is known to be present in the membrane of synaptic vesicles where it drives the import of neurotransmitter molecules via a range of antiporter protein complexes (PMID:28383767). The mammalian V-ATPases consists of a peripheral V1 domain and an integral V0 domain (PMID: 16524922). Two-step differential centrifugation and a high-affinity effector protein SidK was used to purify mammalian V-ATPase subunit homologs from synaptic vesicles of rat brain. SDS-PAGE was used to isolate V-ATPase subunits (Fig1B and S1A) and was subjected to mass spectrometry for subunit identification (Fig1C and S1B). Cryogenic Electron Microscopy was used to yield 3D atomic maps of the V-ATPase V1 (Fig 1D, 2A, S5 and S7) and V0 subunits (Fig3A) and demonstrates that ATP6V1B2 is part of the V1 domain of mammalian V-ATPase.

- Based on the constructed 3D cryogenic electron microscopic model , the "extrinsic component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: Membranes enriched for synaptic vesicles were collected from adult Norwegian rat brains. SidK1-278-3xFLAG solution applied to agarose beads and centrifuged. The flow through was then subjected to SDS-PAGE, mass spectropmetry and cryoEM.
